11093063:
Firewatch Peaks

The Firewatch Peaks are a series of squat bunkers manned by soldiers from Ustalav located in the Tusk Mountains of the Hold of Belkzen, just south of Whisperfall Pass.

In total, there are twelve bunkers in operation, all under the control of the County of Canterwall and with the intention of monitoring orc (and sometimes dragon) activity in the area. The structures themselves predate the inhabitants of Ustalav, however, instead bearing markings of dwarven design.

In addition to underground tunnels, connecting each fortress, individual bunkers can communicate to each other (and the outside world) via trained hawks. More impressive is that in times of attack the stone roofs are capable of being lowered into the ground, making the bunkers virtually impervious to attack. This makes the defenses ideal against the ever-present threat of dragon attacks from Raschka-Tor.

The fortifications are often tested by orc attack, most notably from the nearby Skull Eater tribe.
15290893:
Vezhnu

Vezhnu is a small town on the shore of Rampore Isles in the Shackles. Large herds of livestock graze in the plains surrounding the town, and several slaughterhouses are arranged on the shore. The excess blood from the slaughterhouses drains into the ocean, attracting ravenous sharks.

The Docks of Chastening are used for the punishment, which entails lashings on the docks and—for more serious crimes—being repeatedly dipped beneath the shark-infested waves until the beasts consume the victims.

43612653:
Tomb of the Necrophage

The Tomb of the Necrophage is the mausoleum of a minor aristocrat from Yamasa named Mifutu, but after the country's destruction by the Eye of Abendego, a group of cannibalistic cultists moved into the area and began devouring corpses, setting traps throughout. Unholy symbols of Urgathoa, Rovagug, and Zura fill the chambers, and undead and elementals fight amongst themselves for control. The upper levels are completely flooded, and a small rift between planes is what has allowed elementals to enter the complex, while the rest of the complex is protected by wards and constructs that activate when anyone disturbs the tombs.
43975383:
Lasinavel

Lasinavel is a city situated on the north bank of the Kyonin River about 30 miles upstream from Iadara, famous for its countless feasts. The city was founded by Lasina, a renowned chef who wanted to create a place to host others.

The kitchen is at the heart of Lasinavel culture, where magical processes create an environment in which food and ingredients remain fresh for a long time. Chefs are invited to prepare their dishes in the city's ovens. Although it boasts many festivals, none has become as famous as the feast in which Lasina created a dish resembling a dragon.
82052233:
Delronge Estate

3 locations:

  • Vashnarstill Estate
  • Whitegate Market
85951583:
Deadhaven

Deadhaven is a ghoul settlement located on the remote Island of the Vibrant Dead in the shattered continent of Azlant.
102201783:
Bronze Hook

Bronze Hook is a small trading town located in the foothills of northwestern Katapesh's Brazen Peaks, just a short distance north of the Pale River. Once a stop on a now-defunct caravan route from Ipeq to Solku, the town no longer makes the profits it once did ferrying goods and passengers across the river and charging tolls for the use of the Hook Ford. Since the trade route fell into disuse, the people of Bronze Hook have learned to survive off of what little trade trickles along the old road or the river. With their town's coffers always in mind, the local constabulary has increasingly turned a blind eye as slavers, smugglers, and traders of exotic creatures from the Brazen Peaks bring their goods through the town, despite the moral implications and frequent disturbances that come with them.
102456593:
Torch Orchard

The Torch Orchard is a stunning and very exclusive orchard. It is also secretly a meeting place for followers of Mephistopheles.
105319353:
Commonfield

Commonfield is more of a collection of pesh-farming homesteads in western Katapesh than a town, but it represents the nation's densest concentration of pesh farming. Twenty-two farmers, their families, and their hired hands call Commonfield's 36 farms home. These farmers are, for the most part, hardworking and honest; their crops are precious, requiring them to post guards at night.

The five wealthiest farmers meet weekly to discuss coordinating guard patrols, how the season fares, any insect or rodent pests affecting the crop, and other topics. Two notable members of the council are the aiuvarin Richele Gerst and the halfling Kerek Cullstone.
118786593:
Maggot Tree

3 locations:
  • Bloody Tarn
  • Massacre House
  • Trollclaw Cleft
137132743:
Dhuraxilis

The cosmopolitan Iblydan city of Dhuraxilis is the capital city of the city-state of Pol-Dhuraxilis and is populated by beast-blooded people and is the home of the half-fiend hero-god Iapholi, chosen one of the Ekrian Revival cult.
140681783:
Jarvis Estate

5 locations:

  • Tanessen Estate
  • Victocora Estate
  • Whitegate Market
  • Xerysis Manor
141031343:
Finderplain

Near the center of Katapesh, the small gnome town of Finderplain provides temporary home for travelers exploring the area.

Finderplain has stood 300 years as a way-point for gnomes traveling south of the Inner Sea. It holds almost 2,000 residents, but the constant influx and departure means that the number could change completely every few years. Families rarely establish permanent homes in Finderplain; the lure of adventure and desert beckons, and the gnomes move on, leaving their houses empty for the next travelers. The transitory nature of the gnomes means that no individual consistently rules over Finderplain. The current ruler is a gnome bard named Wexlis Bean.
151215503:
Candlehalls

Candlehalls is one of the two most notorious ruins of noble houses and estates in the blasted wasteland of the Furrows, along with the House of Ensland.

Candlehalls lies a couple of miles from the Kingfisher River, but is today surrounded by the blackened trench system known as the Peasant Graves. The estate is almost certainly haunted, as ghost lights are seen playing around the grounds and other-worldly gamblers frequent the noble houses trying to entrap wanderers across the charred lands.
162067283:
Cettigne

Formerly an independent city-state, Molthune annexed Cettigne in 4708 AR when the city was beset by plague and famine. The nation installed Irina Klim, Molthune's youngest General Lord, as Cettigne's new ruler, but the locals continue to chafe at their new-found servitude. The traditional Free City Citizen Militia continues to protect the city independently of the General Lords. Friction between the locals and the national forces may soon lead to violence.

Cettigne is built around four castles representing the various periods of its history; they are reflected on the city's flag, four white castles on a green field. A fifth castle is under construction, representing Molthuni rule.

Cettigne has a small temple of Abadar.
162416343:
Meravon

Meravon, sometimes spelled Merovan, is a logging settlement located within the Immenwood on the Isle of Kortos. It is considered the center of Absalom's lumber industry, and the wood cut in the forest is transported either by the Deluge River to Diobel or overland to Otari.
180583503:
Lethaquel

Lethaquel is a site in the eastern hills of the elven nation of Kyonin, halfway between the Fierani Forest and the Sellen River.
187759943:
Crowtop

Crowtop is an abandoned village in the Irriseni province of Hoarwood. Inexplicably, the village has not decayed or been buried by the snow. It stands just as it did over a thousand years ago. Nobody knows its original name, but it is now called Crowtop because of the thousands of crows that perch on the village's roofs and fence posts in strange silence. It is one of the few places in Irrisen where crows can be found in large numbers, as they are normally killed on sight.

People say the village's residents suddenly vanished. Within the houses, meals sit unfinished and chores half-completed, but nobody is known to have actually set foot in the place. It is said that Crowtop is cursed and the crows are the souls of all the Ulfen slain in Baba Yaga's conquest. They also say the crows serve some nefarious purpose for the White Witches, while others claim that they are psychopomps.
194666243:
Omash

Omash is a fortress-city in northern Qadira near the border of Taldor that lies on the eastern end of the Satrapy's patrols. The city contains a dozen schools of war from which the satrap personally chooses warriors for special duties. Each of them specializes in a different tactic or troop type: there are schools for horse cavalry, camel cavalry, carpet cavalry, archery, polearms, siege warfare, and castle-storming. Some of the Peerless, the personal advisors to the satrap, have come from these schools.
212666003:
Cho-Tzu

2 locations:
  • Haigui Wan
  • Sanctum of the Sorrowmaker
217908063:
City of Whistling Swords

The City of Whistling Swords is the ruined first capital of the pre-Lung Wa nation of Ootoya, the site of which is now the Successor State of Kwanlai in Tian Xia. This lost city is located in the western foothills of Kwanlai and mostly ignored by its tengu inhabitants.

Still, it draws outsiders and adventurers seeking to loot the city's supposedly untouched armouries, a difficult task since the city is infested with rat kings, oni, and other foul spirits, all led by the hateful underworld dragon Mujihi who was exiled to the surface world that he so despises.
221911033:
Elsekulp

Elsekulp is a large Taldan town on the banks of the Porthmos River, approximately 90 miles upriver from the capital, Oppara.
223324023:
Harse

Founded in 4536 AR by Sarwin Harse, the town of Harse is an important gateway between Korvosa's inland holdings and its seaward settlements. Positioned at the junction of the Sarwin and Falcon Rivers, the town's twin ferries provide the easiest means of crossing both rivers for travelers in the area.

The town is built on a narrow strip of land between the Sarwin and Falcon rivers, and has been a river crossing since the time before the Chelaxian arrived. It contains only a few dozen buildings, including a church of Erastil run by Mother Tasa Seganni, two grocers, and two taverns (the Spotted Pony, and the Nag Bag). Most of the inhabitants do not live in the village proper, but on isolated farms and ranches spread about the local countryside. The town is known for its horse and livestock breeding, and holds a large rodeo at the Harse Market each year to determine Varisia's top riders and animals.
223346633:
Eyes of the Bronze Man

The Eyes of the Bronze Man are part of the Face of the Bronze Man, a bronze-plated monument to Pharaoh Djederet I (died -1653 AR) that is shaped in his likeness. It is located in southern Osirion's Brazen Peaks. Each of the eyes is an entrance to a played-out mine, one of tin and the other of copper, which legend tells were important to the development of bronzeworking in humanity's distant past.
229037953:
Bloodsalt

Bloodsalt is the largest of several ruined cities on the shore of Terwa Lake, at the northern edge of Rechiend's Plains in the Mwangi Expanse. It takes its name from the region's red, iron-rich silt (similar to that which gave Bloodcove its name). Much of the city is now buried beneath the silt. The local tribes view the ruins as taboo. Treasure hunters who survive the creatures living within the ruins also have to escape the wrath of the natives. Few clues remain to identify the city's original inhabitants, but visitors tell of uncovering skeletons whose various locations imply the entire population died at once. In fact, the lake occasionally releases a cloud of deadly volcanic gas, which kills everything in the vicinity. The city is also sometimes known as the City of the Dragon-Men, after iconography found there which depicts people sprouting dragon-like wings and flying off to commune with spirits.
239316403:
Menhemes Manor

The Menhemes Manor stands against the wall facing Absalom in Westgate, the home of one of the city's oldest noble families. House Menhemes has constructed several black pyramids, a corridor lined with sphinx statues, and sand gardens that showcase their lineage to the nation of Osirion, but what is most striking is their extensive library containing archives thousands of years old, managed by the brilliant Scion Lord Rogren Sphairo.

Alkenstar City

254557693:
Dawdlefoot Ranch

The Dawdlefoot Ranch is a sheep ranch founded by a former Chelaxian halfling slave on the Nesmian Plains in Nirmathas. It was used by the Bellflower Network to smuggle slaves out from Cheliax and provide the former slaves with forged paperwork to start new lives. It is run by Hannah Dawdlefoot, the granddaughter of the ranch's founder. The ranch has numerous magical illusions along with mundane disguises and defenses such as thorny, poisonous plants.
260303123:
Fields of Pain's Forgetting

The hidden Fields of Pain's Forgetting in Ombrefell are filled with bio-luminescent, hallucinogenic mushrooms, white poppies, the powerful analgesic flayleaf, and other plants that produce some sort of addictive pain-relieving drug. Some are intentionally poisoned to expose black-market sellers of pain-avoidant substances heretical to Kuthite beliefs, while pure specimens are distributed to religious and independent torturers. Mistress Cultivator Preali Dhat, a representative of the Umbral Court, oversees distribution and fulfills requests.
289501733:
House of Oblivion

In Thuvia's interior desert lies the House of Oblivion. The towering structure was built over 7,000 years ago and was designed to lure Ahriman to Golarion. While the House of Oblivion is primarily built above ground, its various wings do not fully reside in the Universe.
297739043:
Shadow Caverns

The Shadow Caverns are located in Nidal's sinister Uskwood, not far from the capital Pangolais. On the surface they appear to be nothing more than a cave above an icy spring, but they conceal an entrance to the Darklands.
299738283:
Everrest

Everrest is the ruins of a Chelaxian manor house built in the Nesmian Plains of Nirmathas by House Arvanxi before Cheliax lost its claim to Molthune and Nirmathas. Abandoned by House Arvanxi since the Chelaxian Civil War, the current occupants of the ruins are exiles of the Thornscar orc tribe, led by Krov Thirdmother, a fanatical follower of Lamashtu.
314852833:
Nogortha Necropolis

The Nogortha Necropolis (also known as the Necropolis of Nogortha) is a vast burial site located in central Andoran between the Arthfell Mountains and Nogortha Peaks, southwest of the city of Oregent. These barrow mounds are the final resting place of hundreds of barbarians who once inhabited the Arthfell Forest but who were finally defeated in 1707 AR by Taldor's campaign of western expansion led by General Khastalus. Many of the burial mounds have been emptied by grave robbers or are being excavated by Pathfinders, although many also remain to be explored.
326495213:
Castle Issono

Castle Issono is part of the eastern wall of the Chelaxian city of Corentyn. Most foot traffic comes through its gates, passes through Easttown and the Noble Quarters, and accesses the harbor.

The Castle Issono is one of the nine famous forts of Corentyn, according to the classical listing.

334306533:
Icegraves

The Icegraves is the traditional burial site for the leaders of the Varki people in the Linnorm Kingdom of Icemark. After having their brains and entrails removed (which are offered to the sky on a platform), the Varki bury the bodies in a grave melted out of the ice. Anyone who visits the burial site can see countless bodies amidst their burial goods perfectly preserved in the ice.

357520593:
Mukinami

Mukinami (pronounced moo-kee-nah-mee) was once a free city where people from the four provinces that make up modern Songbai could meet and trade freely. Now, it serves as the capital of the recently formed nation. The nation's ruler, the Sun Shogun, governs from the city but in order to try and assuage the former nobility of the realm, he takes advice from a quarrelsome group called the Four Notables—a quartet of aristocratic families that previously ruled Songbai's four provinces.

372358443:
Mantarin

Mantarin was built in the southern Rimethirst Mountains during the First Mendevian Crusade as a penal colony, but after Drezen fell, all contact with Mendev ceased. After realizing the gravity of their situation, the commanders offered all of the prisoners freedom as long as they swore oaths to join the crusade and fight against the demons of the Worldwound in an effort to make it back to Mendev, but all were killed before completing half of the journey.

As of 4711 AR, the penal colony was populated entirely by half-fiends serving their demon masters. It is unknown what state the fortress and its population are in after the closing of the Worldwound.
375875503:
Cindra's Exotic Pets

1 locations:

382354573:
Merishai

Merishai is a town in the Ketz Desert of Qadira.
382403743:
Watchtower

The Watchtower is located at the eastern edge of the city, making it the tallest tower on Absalom, surpassing both the Blue Tower and the Pilot Island lighthouse. From this structure, the Cairnlands are monitored, and even the distant Immenwood can be seen. The tower serves as the Eagle Garrison's base of operations, planning its expeditions before setting out across the island. They are the eyes and ears of the First Guard, searching for smoke to report fires and concerned less with investigations or arrests than with threats beyond a few isolated individuals. There's a running joke among the recruits that the tower isn't just tall, but contains an even deeper dungeon. This isn't true, but the recruits find it amusing to pass on the joke, which the commanders don't take lightly. These might not be just rumors, as a recruit ended up digging in the ground looking for a supposed secret wine cellar, only to find tunnels they didn't want to explore because something was whistling to them in the dark, and they quickly sealed the hole after the incident was reported.
383080173:
Obrand's Stand

Obrand's Stand is a fort with walls that provide approximately a mile of space for archers ready to attack invaders. Throughout Corentyn's history, the fort has never been breached by an invader.
387054063:
Parley Point

The surprisingly welcoming port of Parley Point is on the coast of the Garundi mainland region of the Shackles. It is a popular port of call for merchant captains travelling north, who often stop off here before braving the storms of the Eye of Abendego. It is renowned for its friendly citizens and fine inns (and brothels).

The Parley Point lighthouse warns passing vessels of the nearby reefs and dangerous surf. The crews of those vessels that do run aground are rescued by the town's fleet of galleys.

However, what the sailors fail to realise is that the town also has a second, false lighthouse further inland, that is sometimes used to fool captains into running aground. The citizens rescue these sailors in the same way as the others, but also surreptitiously make off with as much of their cargo as they can salvage.
391734643:
House of Guile

The House of Guile is a gallery in Eastgate where Parsin Guile exhibits his works—some artistic, others more practical, featuring mechanical designs in wood—to the general public. The commercial side is managed by his wife, Hilenda Guile. The building's facade features over 30 different types of wood sourced from the Isle of Kortos, some of which have been extinct for centuries.
392270583:
Castle Clarion

3 locations:
  • Cindra's Exotic Pets
  • Cliffside Tavern
  • Jerg's Repair Shop
393340303:
Hoarwood

The city of Hoarwood lies in the cold, northern nation of Irrisen. Situated at the heart of the Hoarwood Forest, the entire city is carved from the trunk of a petrified tree of incredible size. Its only other claim to fame is the wild debauchery of the constant parties thrown by its ruler, Anelisha Elvanna, and her brother and lover Ghrathis.
399806393:
Dead Man's Dome

Dead Man's Dome is a cairn to a fallen soldier who died defending what used to be a mighty watchtower against a force of giants and undead. It is located far to the north of the continent of Tian Xia on the polar ice of the Crown of the World and overlooks the tundra basin of Ruun Uvas near the Path of Aganhei. The site is said to still be guarded by the spirit of the dead warrior whose name was never known.
402349503:
Radripal

Radripal, also known as the City of Arches, is a Vudran city divided—both physically by the Matra River gorge, and socially by class and caste.
406668923:
Castle Clarion

Castle Clarion was built in Katapesh by Oslynn Clarion, a now-retired explorer, about 4600 AR. A small set of docks, positioned under the cliffs of Castle Clarion, are used by the Clarion family for any shipping they may need to do and can hold nearly all of the Clarion fleet of ships (nearly a dozen blacksailed vessels). The castle, made up of slim towers that rise high above the castle, are connected by covered bridges. The sparkling walls and red roofs of Castle Clarion make it an easy landmark to spot from land or sea. Lord Clarion is loyal to the government of Katapesh and dutifully adheres to all of the city's laws and requests. He is also one of the city's largest merchant of slaves.

Despite its elegance and lack of fortifications, the castle is well defended against Lord Clarion's many enemies, most notably agents of the Gray Corsairs. Traps riddle the castle and his servants include veteran warriors and mages. Also, due to Lord Crarion's ties with the Pactmasters, several aluum guard his property.
433527223:
Pho Yim

Pho Yim, sometimes written Mount Pho-Yim, is an enormous volcano located in the south of Tang Mai that looms over the nation's capital of Surat Prakan and is a part of the vast Kullan Dei mountain range. While Pho Yim is currently not active, it does smoke ominously and it is rumoured that its molten fury is only held at bay by the harmony of the five sorcerous rulers of Tang Mai. Nevertheless, During the Age of Ascendancy, the volcano did erupt explosively destroying much of Tang Mai in 7099 IC and, some say, in consequence of the invasion of the country by Lung Wa some years previously.

The local hero Ankilhi used the fires of Pho Yim to forge a tetsubo out of the remains of the graveknight Pnao Kilnak.
434644603:
Utterhome

Utterhome is the base of operations for the Sleepless Suns, the guard of Absalom's Foreign Quarter. The structure was a manor in the Osirian style, complete with Jadwiga ice statues, glass panels imported from New Thassilon, Tian Xia-style pools, and Vudrani area gardens, the fortress reflects the district's diversity. The very name Utterhome represents the many ways in which languages ​​refer to the concept of home, and all people are welcome regardless of their cultural background. The guard's name also has a similar concept, indicating that the sun never sets for them, as their members come from all over the world. Therefore, when it is night in Absalom, it is day in Tian Xia, and its members embody that devotion to citizen protection.
444045413:
Harvest's End

Harvest's End is a small village located near where the Rimeflow River and the Iceflow River meet in the Bleakmarch region of Irrisen. The village mainly sustains itself from fishing and trade, with the traveling merchants standing out sharply with their colorful clothes next to drab woolen clothes of the villagers. The town also has a ferry for merchants and lumberers to cross the Rimeflow River operated by an ice troll named Rimetusk.

Rimetusk serves as the de facto leader of the town after allowing his ego to grow too large, since the town's isolation from Whitethrone means that Rimetusk communicates infrequently with the Jadwiga to whom he is supposed to report.
462722923:
Hanpa

Hanpa is a town in the Ketz Desert of Qadira.
467552523:
Magilloch Ranch

The Magilloch Ranch is a goat ranch on the Nesmian Plains of Nirmathas. It was originally built by the Magilloch family after the Shining Crusade and has remained in the same hands since then. Unbeknownst at the time, Willow Magilloch, a paladin, was infected by a wasting illness that eventually turned her into a vampire. A few undead-blooded sorcerers appear in the Magilloch family every generation. As of 4717 AR Gran Willow, her family, and vampire spawn created from passing Molthuni soldiers have created a massive tunnel complex beneath their ranch.
469768253:
Lilywhite

Lilywhite is a small pirate town on the western coast of Motaku Isle in the Shackles that is home to a large number of worshipers of Cayden Cailean. It is the smallest of Motaku's settlements and was founded in 4682 AR by two pirates, Delemona Burie and Shaggard Halleck, devotees of Cayden Cailean. Alongside the foundation of the town, the pair funded the construction of a grand stone temple to the Drunken Hero. Halleck was killed in an alcohol-fueled duel in 4692 AR. Halleck's son, Little Shaggard, and Burie are the town's pirate lords.
494502713:
Xin-Grafar

504934993:
Citadel Dinyar

Citadel Dinyar was the base of operations for the Order of the Godclaw Hellknights under Lictor Resarc Ountor. The citadel was seized by the Iomedaean Glorious Reclamation in 4715 AR.
505782153:
Selkelas

An enormous headless stone torso known as Selkelas is half-buried near the western edge of the Katapesh desert. Its size indicates that, had it been a fully detailed humanoid statue, it would have measured at least 20 feet in height. The name "Selkelas" is engraved on the statue's back. One of its granite hands seems to have been carved to hold a now-missing weapon, perhaps a sword.
519950953:
Temple of the Elk

523135623:
Osibu

The streets of the southeastern city of Osibu are literally paved with gold. Located in the Screaming Jungle within the greater Mwangi Expanse, the city is protected by a ring of statues called the Circle of Twice-Honored Women, which somehow reduces the cacophony of noise from the jungle and also keeps out the jungle's carnivorous plant life. Osibu is incredibly well hidden, with many maps not even acknowledging its existence, or misleading readers to another city or ruin within the Mwangi Expanse. Finding the city is an ordeal that involves many trials with the dangerous flora and fauna along the way.

The city is a democracy, and life is a veritable utopia for its inhabitants. There are plentiful supplies of fruit and water, and the city's wise-women are able to mix rare herbs to produce an ointment which greatly extends life and staves off diseases.

Osibu is home to possibly Golarion's oldest living creature, the hunched arboreal regent Dimari-Diji, Final Tree of the Elder World. The ancient arboreal regent's first memory is the impact of Earthfall nearly ten thousand years ago. Dimari-Diji guards the Nemesis Well, a way between worlds.
529556233:
Caves of the Craven

The Caves of the Craven are an enormous network of damp caves and tunnels located beneath the Calphiak Mountains of western Varisia. The caves' main inhabitants are countless tribes of goblins who raid the nearby human communities of the Velashu River valley. The tunnels eventually lead down to Nar-Voth and provide the most direct route from the surface to the xulgath realm of Kuvhoshik. The goblins of the caves fear the xulgaths and generally keep out of their affairs.

The caves also have a connection to ancient history. They were the escape route for a group of elves from the nearby city of Celwynvian in the Mierani Forest, who chose to escape the imminent impact of the Starstone by fleeing deep underground in -5293 AR. Although this exodus saved them from the worst ravages of Earthfall, they encountered dangers in the Darklands they had never expected. One such access point to the caves is from the ruins of the Tower of the Ironwood Watch located on the Velashu Uplands.
531460753:
Winterbreak

The city of Winterbreak is located on Acuben Isle in Brevoy on the Lake of Mists and Veils. The city is controlled by House Lodovka . To the city's south-east is located Winterbreak Bay.
541871953:
Temple of the Elk

1 locations:

543091053:
Cavlinor

Cavlinor is an elven outpost in Nirmathas, located on the southern bank of the Deepcut River where it joins the Marideth River. A few dozen mostly transient elves live in this settlement, which resembles a hunting lodge more than an actual town. The elves are mostly concerned with exploring elven ruins in the area and making diplomatic contact with the fey of the Fangwood. Sivron Nal, Cavlinor's manager, was an outspoken advocate for aiuvarins in Kyonin, and their appointment here—far from the other elves—might be construed as exile.
558166173:
Pantheon of Many

Broken Bastion

  • Gallery of Innovation
588080173:
Dustpawn

Dustpawn is a small town in eastern Isger, about seven miles from the Conerica River and close to the border with Druma, and was founded in 4324 AR.
589375853:
Absalom

  • Copperwood
  • Crimson Coin

594337563:
Pillars of Kreth

The Pillars of Kreth are located on the plains just west of the Flood Road in northern Belkzen. The Pillars are a series of eleven dolmens—massive vertical standing stones topped with flat lintels. The site was discovered by the orc Kreth but the orcs are superstitious by nature and they have never revisited the Pillars of Kreth since then. The first ten dolmens are each between 8 and 9 feet in height, 3 feet wide and 2 feet thick. The linels are 3 feet thick, overhanging the stones beneath them by approximately a foot on all sides. The easternmost dolmen is only around 4 feet high. The stones seem to have been worked by primitive hand tools and contain numerous symbols and pictograms that correspond to no known language. Some of the symbols are clearly stars, comets, and planets. The eleven dolmens forms an irregular tunnel that once a year aligns with the red planet Akiton. During this time the sky above the Pillars turns strange colors, and a vibration can be felt from half a mile away. As the vibrations become stronger, a torus of churning auroral light rises a mile above the site and a pulse of energy passes through the first ten dolmens and strikes the top of the easternmost one. Anything on top of the last dolmen dissapears for ever.

Pathfinders who have studied the Pillars of Kreth believe that they could be a kind of interplanetary transportation device, similar to the Doorway to the Red Star in the Mwangi Expanse or the Maze of the Open Road in Galt.
605800183:
Arena

Arena is a small town on the northeastern coast of Widowmaker Isle in the Shackles. It gets its name from the enormous gladiatorial arena that occupies the center of the settlement. This arena is home to some of the most competitive and violent blood sports in the region.
606923003:
Shiman-Sekh

Shiman-Sekh is the northwestern-most city of Osirion, located about 100 miles inland of the Inner Sea on the western edge of the great Golden Oasis, and along a trade route connecting Osirion with its western neighbor of Thuvia.
611500093:
Folarth

The town of Folarth is located in southern Nirmathas near the Deepcut River.
613458513:
Kin Gate

The Kin Gate is located in the northeast of Absalom. It was originally a siege castle, commissioned by the fey prince Juroveal during the Siege of Kin, but when he was banished, the fortification was merged with the rest of the city.
623808143:
Grundlescorn Lair

The Rovagug-worshipping hag sisters Grava, Mopple, and Polnivaar Grundlescorn dwell in a marshy section of Ravounel Forest known as Grundlescorn Lair, in a ramshackle hut partially built into the boughs of an ancient oak tree and supported by several crooked but sturdy stilts. The hut overlooks a low hillock in the marsh atop which the hags keep their cauldron and fire pit.
625372083:
Seidoyaji

Seidoyaji (pronounced say-doh-yah-jee) is one of the many cities that dot the tengu kingdom of Kwanlai. The thieves' guild of Seidoyaji is responsible for the Autumn of Iron Tears, a conflict with the thieves' guild of the city of Todorokaze that escalated into a full-blown proxy war between the two cities.
627042063:
Tolsen Peak

Tolsen Peak is a mountain in the southwestern Menador Mountains of Cheliax east of Hellmouth Gulf.
646545553:
Chastel

Chastel is the largest town in the Ustalavic county of Lozeri, lying on that county's southern border. The town is located close to the southern edge of the Shudderwood just north of the meeting point of the Vhatsuntide River with its tributary, the Vistear River. Chastel has the air of a frontier settlement despite its size, possibly due to the roughness exhibited by the locals. The town, though, does have a large market, lumber mills, and stockyards but lacks any protective walls, and as such, it is often the target of predators, the most notorious being the Rats of Karneik. In 4701 AR, Chastel was invaded by the murderous winter wolf known as the Devil in Gray; in a single night, he killed nearly twenty people before disappearing back into the wilds of Lozeri.
656315763:
Al-Bashir

Al-Bashir is a harpy-infested ruin and oasis in the central Pashman district of Qadira, located on the edge of the Zho Mountains near the Pashman River. It is avoided by the caravans that pass through the mountains. Despite attempts by the Qadiran government, the hundreds of harpies who roost there have never been driven off enough to explore the vast ruins and recover the substantial treasure believed to be within. The songs of the creatures have drawn entire herds of antelopes and camels to their doom.
664328223:
Cassomir

Cassomir is located at the mouth of the Sellen River, and is the second largest settlement in the empire of Taldor. It acts as a major trade city, and through its ports enter goods from the Inner Sea, as well as from nations to the north that utilize the Sellen as a trade route. Cassomir is also home to the greatest of Taldor's imperial shipyards, even though the majority of the fleet itself is stationed in Oppara.
670937783:
Sturnidae

The town of Sturnidae is located in the south of the Ustalavic county of Barstoi, very close to the blasted wasteland known as the Furrows. Its proximity to the Furrows is never forgotten as, when the wind blows aright, a pall of grey ash from the wasteland is blown over the town; it lands on the fields tainting the crops and the food with decay. Furthermore, locals report seeing visions of the war in the clouds of dust, such as marching peasant soldiers and charging knights.
685389833:
Meilin and Zhen Ships

Meilin and Zhen Ships is a shipyard run by natives of Tian Xia who repair ships after long ocean voyages, such as those departing from or arriving from Goka. They build ships in the style of their continent, as these remain popular with the city's nobility.
694620513:
Tree of the Thousand Eyes

The Tree of the Thousand Eyes is a colossal tree located in the south of the Jolizpan Forest in Xopatl. The tree is named for the many patterns covering it that closely resemble the eyes of a peacock's tail.

Those who live nearby believe that the Tree of the Thousand Eyes grew from the heart of the chicome coatl that died when a fragment of the Aeon Star struck it. This legendary event occurred thousands of years ago during the formation of the Jolizpan Forest. According to local legend, the heart of the chicome coatl still exists at the centre of the Tree of the Thousand Eyes and, if someone could make this heart beat again, it would resurrect the powerful coatl.
715598163:
Embassy Building

4 locations:
  • Billivin's Benevolent Balms and Effulgent Elixirs
  • Mistress of Angels
  • Pug's Contraptions
  • Rose and Rake Theater
723727713:
Solku

The large town of Solku is located near the western mountains of Katapesh (the Barrier Wall mountains), where it is relatively cut off from the rest of the nation. The settlement is frequently besieged by kholo slavers, and thus rests within a tall wall, though this does not always provide enough protection to keep the townspeople safe. Today the town is guarded by a small band of paladins of Sarenrae known as the Dawn Vigil who prepare for the rumored war ahead, as kholo amass in the mountains under the banner of Noor, the Red Sultana, who has her eyes set on Solku as the starting point of a massive campaign eastward.
741251133:
Merev

Merev is a city on Yanimere Island, off the western coast of Qadira across from the Ketz Desert.
769976273:
Xiwu

The fortress city of Xiwu, one of the most populated settlements of Tian Xia—or indeed, on Golarion—is the capital of militaristic Lingshen. The city appears to be a military installation unlike anything else in the world.
772220253:
District Courthouse

The District Courthouse in The Docks handles cases brought by both the energetic and rough dockworkers and the reckless and unwary foreigners who don't even know the city's laws. The court system has a reputation for being quite chaotic and unpredictable.
777674693:
Ayin Lun

Ayin Lun is a long-lost ruined city in northern Mendev that had been overridden by undead in generations past. Survivors of the city erased all records of the city after its destruction, and its suspected site was revealed only after the death of Gorum in 4724 AR.

Possibly during the Godsrain that ensued, nine stony spires erupted across Mendev, each containing a gap at several hundred feet of elevation that purportedly lead to strange lands of hostile beings. These towers became known among Mendevians as Godsfall towers.

As of 4725 AR, the towers' purpose and origins are unknown, and Mendevian Chancellor Irahai had officially prohibited climbing the towers and posted guards at each. The towers nonetheless remained scaled by young native Mendevians who consider them to be rites of passage, and many have successfully returned from their attempts.
777677273:
Spindlehorn

A Thassilonian relic from ancient times, the Spindlehorn is a fantastically tall structure located in the Wyvern Mountains of central Varisia. Thousands of feet in height, it rises from the shores of the Storval Deep, contradicting all natural laws of architecture and engineering. It is completely featureless except for a winding stair that winds like a corkscrew around its length, terminating in an open platform ten feet in diameter. The view from this platform is easily one of the most terrifying views in all of Golarion.

To most, the purpose of the spire has been lost to time, although it is told that dark-robed figures can be seen ascending, but never descending its heights from time to time. However, there is an epic poem Jandelay, inscribed at the base of the Spindlehorn, that implies that Jandelay is place of green fields and spires somewhere within the chaos of the Maelstrom, and that a being called the Oliphaunt of Jandelay is Jandelay's eternal guardian. So, some have surmised that Spindlehorn was constructed as a platform from which one could summon the Oliphaunt of Jandelay and command it while making eye contact with the massive creature.

Shamans of the Shoanti Shundar-Quah hold the Spindlehorn sacred, making pilgrimages there once every ten years to discuss tribal matters of import. Rumors suggest a powerful being is buried beneath the tower's base, and the site remains a powerful point for summoning monstrously large fiends.
780490503:
Radish patch

An arrow-shaped glade near the northeastern edge of the Narlmarch Woods, roughly 16 miles south of Oleg's Trading Post, is home to a sizable radish patch, specifically moon radishes. These spicy root vegetables are the main ingredient in moon radish soup—Oleg Leveton's favorite meal. The patch is also occasionally plundered by local kobolds, who consider even uncooked moon radishes a delicacy.
782100993:
Kassen

Kassen is a small town on the Tourondel River in the Fangwood of Nirmathas. It is a relatively close-knit settlement with origins that stretch back to the year 4522 AR, when the town's founder, Ekat Kassen, grew tired of life as an adventurer and retired there.
784857553:
Saringallow

The Isgeri town of Saringallow is located on the Conerica River, south of the capital of Elidir.
785357693:
Black Ink Lake

Black Ink Lake is a frigid yet roiling lake of iron gall ink located on the side of Arazlant Mox on the Isle of Kortos at an elevation of 13,000 feet. It is believed to be the final resting place of Norgorber's mortal effects before his ascension to godhood.

Legends suggest the lake's distinctive composition comes from disposed documents describing Norgorber's crimes. The Court of Black Paper operates a shrine on its shores that uses the lake's ink in occult documents, and for submerging new advocates in occasionally fatal initiations.

Norgorberites make pilgrimages to the site to sacrifice secrets of their own into its depths. Divers who attempt to retrieve items from it find it not only impossibly cold, deep, and dark to navigate, but also sense forces that seem bent on taking hold of them.

Parts of stone ruins submerged within the lake are visible on its surface, and a small stone island is positioned at its center.
796804133:
Tomb of the Bound Sphinx

The Tomb of the Bound Sphinx is a pyramid shaped tomb constructed in the Barrier Wall mountains. It was constructed in -1116 AR by Pharaoh Kenaton to be his final resting place, but weas unknowingly built over an underground chamber into which it collapsed a few decades after construction. This destroyed some of the treasure it contained and shifted some of its passages, making exploration difficult.

As well as the final resting place for Kenaton, the tomb also contains a magical prison which contains Hephtethnet the Reaver, a hieracosphinx who terrorized Osirion during Kenaton's life.
800054443:
Steirwood

The Steirwood is a small rotting forest on the Raugsmauda's Reach island in the Shackles. The forest was once home to many animals but now hosts the majority of the hordes of undead that aimlessly roam the island. Raugsmauda creates her necromantic horrors using victims she captures on her monthly hunts. Some of the terrible creatures that wander Steirwood resemble animals that perhaps once populated the isle or distant lands, including bovine ghasts, elephantine skeletons, and serpentine ghouls. The winding tainted trees spontaneously animate their barbed roots to gore and consume zombie animals that wander too close.
807761133:
Korvosa

2 locations:
  • University of Korvosa
  • Wise Dragon Inn
808778903:
Temple of the Ravenous Moon

The Temple of the Ravenous Moon, originally just called the Temple of the Moon, is in the Terwa Uplands, in an area considered to lie within the Shackles. It is an ancient ziggurat constructed of a red-veined white stone.

It gets its name from the carvings on its walls, which show scenes of mass sacrifices under a grinning, befanged moon. The savage kuru tribes which live in the region avoid the temple.

The Hurricane King, leader of the Shackles Pirates, has sent two missions to investigate the temple, but neither of them returned. It is rumoured that the Pathfinder Society may be preparing to send its own expedition from Nantambu; plus Hemdak Wavebaiter has called for adventurers to explore the temple in an attempt to garner himself favour with the Hurricane King.
818631523:
Muschkal Sepulcher

Muschkal Sepulcher is a shoanti burial site in the Iron Peaks dedicated to the heroes of their people who fought against the Thassilonian practices of slavery. It is a place of pilgrimage for the Skoan-Quah, who go there every few years to consecrate it and pray for their ancestors. In 4719 AR, a gnome pathfinder found the location, raided it, and reported it as a Thassilonian ruin in the Pathfinder Chronicles. When the shoanti clans learned of this, they called an emergency meeting to decide whether to expel the Pathfinder Society from their lands altogether, becoming their enemies. However, they were able to reach an agreement and avoid severing relations.
820933273:
Alma's Ruin

4 locations:

  • Crow's Tooth
  • Redwake
  • Spirit of the Hill
821109873:
Woodsedge

The home of one of the fathers of the revolution, Darl Jubannich (who wrote the damning book On Government that helped start the Red Revolution), Woodsedge has always served as the traditional home for dissident thought and radical philosophies in Galt. Even with the country in its current state, nothing has changed. No matter who is currently in power, one can almost guarantee that the opposition to them will begin in Woodsedge, and any visitor is almost certain to find some sort of dissident movement afoot.
834452303:
Gatefoot

Gatefoot sits as the western end of the Pillar Wall in the Korvosan district of the Heights. The massive foot is one of the remnants of ancient Thassilon that still dot Korvosa and was clearly part of some ancient wall. Many scholars think it most likely also serves as part of some sort of enormous gate.
836962583:
Butraf

Butraf is one of the numerous large, prosperous cities that dot the fertile Qadiran region of Pashman. It is located a few miles south of the banks of the Jackal River; the river forms Pashman's northern border with the neighbouring region of the Plains of Paresh.
841436743:
Darkgate

The Darkgate is a fissure in the Precipice Quarter, created during the earthquake that devastated much of the district. Months after it opened, undead and monsters emerged from the fissure and still roam the city streets. Taking advantage of the situation, the Nailfists moved into the area and keep the threats from below at bay. The gang doesn't do this out of charity, of course; they only allow access to the caverns if they are paid a price.
851692503:
Mistwatch

Mistwatch is a semi-ruined stone castle located atop a granite cliff overlooking the Verduran Fork east of the Isle of Arenway, in the Verduran Province of Taldor.
883147453:
Hawk's Nest

Hawks' Nest is the fortress-headquarters of the Daggermark Assassins' Guild in the River Kingdoms.
890234783:
Cahshil

2 locations:
  • First Storm Kindler Colony
  • Guughwa's Waterfall
892913573:
Crystal Palace

The Crystal Palace is a structure in Mudhaven where the Dod's Filchers operate. Its founder was the aiuvarin Malina Dod, who led the group, but after her mysterious disappearance, a pair of halfling twins took control. It's difficult to keep everyone safe and fed, and the group is taking increasingly dangerous actions to raise money.

896897143:
Overlook

The Overlook serves as a palace, prison, and city hall for the city of Greengold. Multiple towers are connected by suspension bridges, giving an impression of magnificence to ships approaching the city.

Red Mountain
