906414623:
Ovorikheer Pass

Ovorikheer Pass is part of the Path of Aganhei as it descends from the high ice of the Crown of the World into the catchment basin of Ruun Uvas in Tian Xia.
945006193:
Turvik

Turvik is a small fortified city in the north of the Thanelands in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings on the Path of Aganhei.
947090703:
Isa

Isa is a Qadiran town in the south of the Maharev region located on the banks of the Meraz River where the river acts as the border between Maharev and the Meraz Desert region. The Maharev region is one of the safest and most fertile regions in all Qadira.
956055153:
Danjing

Danjing is a vast metropolis in the eastern reaches of the Successor State of Lingshen and its inhabitants number almost 100,000 people. It is located near a unique, massive, impenetrable, unnaturally perfect pillar of resonant, humming crystal that predates the known history of Tian Xia. The crystal's mysteries have drawn large numbers of nagaji, magic users, and alchemists to the site, making the city one of Lingshen's most diverse.
959447623:
Dallo

The village of Dallo sits in the western pan-handle of the nation of Wanshou where it nestles against the nearby neighbouring nation of Zi Ha and the looming Kelsang Mountains. Dallo is located almost as far from the storm-tossed coastline of Wanshou as possible, meaning the influence of Wanshou's evil kraken overlord, Zhanagorr, is as minimal as it can be. Still, Dallo is a village suffused with poverty. It is home to superstitious rice farmers, many of whom worship Malikeen, a samsaran who was a previous incarnation of Bakten Shengrah, who is said to have saved the village from flooding. Malikeen oversaw the construction of the most prominent feature of Dallo, it enormous dam. A stupa dedicated to Malikeen sits atop this massive dam and the waters it contains are used to feed the surrounding rice paddies.
960558413:
Lost Lane

Lost Lane is a street in Rag's End, one of Magnimar's poorest neighborhoods in the city's Beacon's Point district. It is also the location of the headquarters of Magnimar's premier thieves' guild: the Night Scales.
969882193:
Desolation Blade Mounts

The Desolation Blade Mounts are a set of mysterious mounds of hardened clay. No one knows who made them, but visitors often return with stone markers marked with a symbol of a pale hand and blackened blade on them.
970130073:
Kallow Mounds

The Kallow Mounds are the burial grounds of the Shoanti clan known as the Skoan-Quah, and are located on the Storval Plateau approximately 50 miles east of Kaer Maga.
971452653:
Monolith Glacier

The Monolith Glacier is located near the north-westernmost corner of the Thanelands in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings; it is formed in the western reaches of the high Stormspear Mountains range and is slowly moving southwards. The Monolith Glacier has a distinct, very symmetrical oblong shape and those few brave explorers who have ventured up onto the glacier report seeing a large fortress entombed within the very heart of the glacier. Monolith Glacier is a very dangerous place to explore: blizzards often engulf it; its surface is riddled with crevasses and ice tunnels created by the numerous deadly remorhazes that live in the glacier's depths. Unbeknownst to everyone, the glacier is a sentient elemental that speaks with a voice of grinding ice and might be willing to allow access to the strange structure at its heart to anyone who can rid it of the pesky remorhazes infesting it.
992015113:
Coralesian

The small town of Coralesian sits high in the northeastern Five Kings Mountains, and even though it is considered to be within the territory of the dwarves, it has been settled by mostly elves from neighboring Kyonin, who live here among the snowcapped peaks and pine trees of the mountains. Some live in small huts in the high alpine meadows, while others have built cliff-dwellings connected by ladders, bridges, and tunnels. No matter where they make their home, however, they all band together to support each other through the long, cold mountain winters.
999200093:
Lady's Light

2 locations:
  • Solifugid Nest
  • Temple of Gogunta
1018652403:
Shimmerford

Shimmerford is a town in the River Kingdoms known for legends of the cryptid Mosquito Witch, in whose name the town stages an annual festival.
1019403543:
Clicking Caverns

The Clicking Caverns are set of underground tunnels and caverns known to connect to the Darklands of the continent of Tian Xia and are located on the border between the nations of Xa Hoi and Nagajor. They are inhabited by flesh-eating clockwork constructs haunted by the souls of their extinct builders.
1019481123:
Darinkhuur

The ancient hold of Darinkhuur is a town in the Erutaki confederation of Yumyzyl, built in the forested foothills of the Wall of Heaven. Though Kyzuv is Yumyzyl's de facto capital, Darinkhuur is its cultural center and has a higher permanent population. All members of the Yumyzyl tribe make occasional pilgrimages to Darinkhuur's spirit-lodges, where the nation's largest canoes are built and receive blessings.
1021551653:
Canter House

Canter House was once the seat of the Ustalavic noble family of House Canter of Vieland. It burnt to the ground in 4399 AR, revealing a black, featureless, egg-like monument made of stone around which the building had been constructed. None of the Canter descendants admitted to having known about this nearly 33-foot-tall monument, and it remains a mystery to this day. Canter House was never rebuilt and it remains a blackened ruin to this day, sometimes known as the Throne Between Sun and Stars.
1026414263:
Andos Hall

4 locations:
  • Highwall Market
  • Nidalese embassy
  • Tarry Imp
  • Three-Legged Devil
1027541483:
Gold Manticore Fount

The Gold Manticore Fount is located in one of the poorest neighborhoods of Pangolais. The fountain has a reputation for granting wishes to people with pure hearts who are in immediate need. It is said that the fountain has helped homeless people, children, and lonely souls in their greatest times of need. The truth is that the fountain itself is not magical; the one granting wishes is Astarathian, a gold dragon with a human identity, who considers the problems he hears and either hires help for those in need or assists them himself.
1028579953:
Samsara Oasis

Samsara Oasis is a small town established by calikangs horrified at their role in the destruction of the region. They use Samsara Oasis as a place to meet and research restoration efforts to try to make up for this.
1030127323:
Starstone Cathedral

Arguably the most sacred location in all of Golarion, the Starstone Cathedral is a monument to divinity. The massive structure was created by Aroden when he raised the Starstone from the depths of the Inner Sea in 1 AR, and the relic is believed to rest within to this day.
1044248203:
Vira Julistarc

Vira Julistarc is a group of structures under the same crystalline roof, a Taldan defensive tradition maintained by House Julistarc, in addition to more modern advancements.
1054141143:
Humbolt's Outfitters

6 locations:
  • Flying Alderman
  • Foreign Quarter Council Hall
  • Greenstar Market
  • Guiding Hand
  • Rhet's Home
  • Yemhasin
1055368963:
Grand Pass

The small city of Grand Pass is located on the cusp of the Mildanesi Mountains, next to the pass that links Xopatl with the neighbouring nation of Innazpa. During the height of the Razatlan Empire, Grand Pass was little more than a watch post but, after the empire's fall, the soldiers who once manned the border-post established the city of Grand Pass. Nowadays, Grand Pass is mostly known for being home to the most renowned fighting school in Xopatl. This school holds a biannual tournament that concludes with a spectacular battle atop the nearby Panischo Mountain, the tournament is so well known that it draws combatants from across the whole of Arcadia.
1058303013:
Cathedral of Obalas

The Cathedral of Obalas is a massive structure inhabited the ancient sorcerer, Obalas. Hundreds of years ago, he angered basically every major player in the nearby capital of Chillblight, who declared him a renegade and forced him to flee. Together with his air and ice elemental slaves, they settled here and built this cathedral, where he still lives today.
1061491353:
Black Whale

Established in 4693 AR, the Black Whale is a prison on the western part of the Flotsam Graveyard intended for the most notorious or politically sensitive prisoners too dangerous to be kept in the Brine in Puddles.

The prison consists of six beached ships, bolted to it with long chains and each lashed together. The guards and other staff stay on the northernmost of the ships, which holds their quarters, all the rowboats, the armory, and the storerooms. An adjacent ship is home to the galley and processing rooms where prisoners are fed and readied for the other ships. A series of pulleys connects ropes and anchors to all six ships, and during rough seas, the prisoners push a pulley axle on each ship to tow supply ships in.

Due to the nature of these criminals, only the Grand Council holds a complete list of who resides within the prison, and few survive long in the squalid conditions aboard (although death typically occurs only due to natural causes). The Lord Warden of the Black Whale is the Fourth Lord of Laws, Guirden of House Gixx. Due to the importance of these prisoners, guards are ever watchful of prisoners breaking out, or outsiders breaking in.

Acting Primarch Wynsal Starborn has proposed overhauling the Black Whale and replacing its leadership due to reports of corruption within the prison; corruption he witnessed firsthand when he was wrongly imprisoned there.
1092877473:
Menador Gap

The Menador Gap is the sole passage through the Menador Mountains and Ravounel Forest, connecting the northwestern corner of the Archduchy of Menador to southeastern Ravounel in Cheliax. Part of the gap is a treacherous stretch of narrow mountainside roads constructed long ago by dwarven engineers, with Menador Keep located at the roads' narrowest point.
1105558373:
The Uskheart

The Uskheart is the central redoubt of the infamous Nidalese circle of druids called the Shades of the Uskwood. Located in the southern Uskwood, the Shades have recently uncovered a disturbing life form known as the hive. These creatures consume and transform everything they encounter, and they seem to be outside the natural order of Golarion. Despite their uniqueness, they are not extraplanar or supernatural in origin and refer to themselves by an unpronounceable Aklo word.
1112765213:
Gorum's Tankard

Gorum's Tankard is a dark lake in the foothills of the Stormspear Mountains in the north of the Thanelands in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. It is the setting for tales about the hero Bjorlan and his enemy, the dragon witch Aservalg. As of 4711 AR, the lake was guarded by dwarven druids.
1115283613:
Monkey's Paw

The Monkey's Paw is a 60-foot-tall statue in the shape of a simian forearm and six-fingered hand located on the northern shores of Lake Ocota in the Mwangi Expanse. The tips of each of the fingers is shaped like the face of a different species of ape, cleverly hidden in the folds of the fingerprints. The eyes of the apes seem to follow one around as one moves around the statue. Ancient glyphs surround the base of the statue that are written in an ancient hybrid of the Chthonian and Mwangi languages. A secret door in the statue leads one into a small religious shrine dedicated to the demon lord Angazhan.
1115833613:
Trezira

Trezira is a small town on the Frozen Road River in the eastern part of Irrisen's Bleakmarch province. It single distinguishing feature is that it was granted a monopoly on "ice cleaving" (the process of keeping a river navigable year-round) the Frozen Road in 4623 AR by Queen Elvanna. Nearly a hundred years later, fur-wrapped citizens of Trezira still patrol the river in their "breaker barges", using any means at their disposal to maintain a navigable channel through the ice.

The ruler of the town is Baroness Marcia who is not very oppressive of the peasants provided they do whatever is necessary to fulfill their obligations and especially do nothing to endanger the "ice cleaving" monopoly. Most successful of the seven "ice cleaving" companies is the White Stewards led by Trepek Gorny. Trezira is also the main trading partner of the frost giant hold of Holvirgang. The frost giant guests in town usually prefer the Horned Helm Inn run by the Ulfen Ludmil Arronsson, a giant of a man himself.
1116901493:
Hall of Commerce

The Hall of Commerce is a magnificent building, constructed in the most modern and ostentatious style, which serves as the government office of the Coin Council as well as a bank. The various district taxes are administered here, and both the Foreign Coin Exchange and the church of Abadar maintain several offices in the building. It is one of the areas with the highest concentration of foreign visitors.
1117582713:
Souston

Souston is a small Andoren fishing village on the Inner Sea, approximately 240 miles due east of the capital of Almas. The town consists of small, brightly colored shacks that cling to the gently sloping coast.
1119529023:
Dretha's Cradle

Dretha's Cradle serves as the home and laboratory for the orc alchemist known as Mother Chot and her apprentices.
1129029273:
Bodie's Haunt

Bodie's Haunt was once a settlement in the River Kingdoms, in what is now called the Protectorate of the Black Marquis.

Up until 4707 AR, it was home to one of the three rival Marquises vying for control of the Pirate Pact. However, in that year Urdal Bazzak arranged for the inhabitants to be slaughtered by Numerian soldiers as his first step to gaining control of the Protectorate.

Today, the town is a ruin haunted by the angry souls of the slain pirates.
1135886863:
Vilelock

Vilelock is a shady, lawless settlement on the Shark Island in the Shackles. As expected, gory brawls, lurking cutpurses, and casual murders are frequent in town.

A thriving market in Vilelock specializes in the acquisition and sale of exotic poisons and alchemical substances outlawed in the more civilized nations of the Inner Sea region.

The town changes overlords often but its current overlord is Nisia Gbele, the Mwangi captain of the frigate Bloody Cudgel.
1137001163:
Dragonspring

The Dragonspring is located in the Darkmoon Plain in a thermal zone rich in copper, iron, and cobalt. Geysers abound in the region, spouting fiery water of varying colors depending on the mineral concentration. A dragon comes to the springs once or twice a year to bathe, then disappears, and no one has ever managed to speak to it or learn its name.
1140951853:
Fenworth Gameporium

Fenworth Gameporium is a card and deck factory that creates, locates, and distributes various games throughout the city, being popular among the casinos and nobles of Absalom who are always looking for new forms of entertainment.
1144940803:
Zom Kullan

Zom Kullan, the capital city of Nagajor, is located inside a volcano deep in the Kullan Dei mountain range. The volcano Zom Kullan is built around is still active and it is believed that it is only through rituals conducted by the royal naga queen, First Mother Vassath Shethagri and her naga royal family that stop the volcano's fury and prevent its eruption.
1146148653:
Glass Pyramids

The Glass Pyramids are a pair of transparent pyramids located in the Inner City district of Katapesh. The pyramids are made of transparent metal plates, a unique design created by Gezbria and Tralvar, the two dwarven wizards interred within who had arrived to Katapesh in 2219 AR.

Unknown to almost all, the two wizards discovered a confluence of magic ley lines beneath the Golden City and built two structures atop it, but too late realised that the magic was corrupt and uncontrollable. To stop this powerful magic being used for evil they built the Glass Pyramids on top of their previous structures, and after warding themselves with powerful magic they interred themselves alive within the pyramids. The magic of the corrupted ley lines soon overwhelmed them, transforming them into evil liches.

The site is regularly investigated by curious adventurers and scholars, but those smart enough to bypass the magic wards inevitably fall victim to the evil liches dwelling within. The Glassclaw tribe of kobolds consider it their sacred mission to ensure that no one ever breaks the Glass Pyramids, claiming to have guarded the structures for more than 100 generations, a longer span of time than the pyramids have existed.
1153530993:
Plumetown

Plumetown is the only major settlement on the island of the Smoker in the Shackles. Willa Cloudbuster is the de facto mayor of the town, and oversees Plumetown's sometimes impatient and disruptive visitors with a small army of constables: former pirates who carry massive clubs. The entire town seems designed solely to house, feed and entertain its visitors who seek the wisdom of the mysterious soothsayers, Keeba's Eyes, living in the caverns of Mount Keeba. It boasts a large number of taverns, hostels, gaming houses, and brothels.

In 4717 AR Whark won the Free Captains' Regatta and was granted this town which she works to modernize into a hub of trade and entertainment.
1177307103:
High Harbor

High Harbor is a once-grand settlement reduced to ruins located on the northeast coast of the Scrape region of the Isle of Kortos.
1178842443:
Westcrown

For 800 years, Westcrown was the capital of Cheliax, and the rumored birthplace and home of the god Aroden. Known as the "City of Nine Stars", it was a shining example of the greatness of the Chelaxian empire. Westcrown was a classic, enlightened metropolis, even a rival to Absalom as a destination of religious pilgrimage and tourism. But then tragedy struck: Aroden died in 4606 AR, a bloody civil war swept over the nation, and decades of turmoil and strife plunged the city into despair.

The ascension of the House Thrune to the Chelaxian throne in 4640 AR cemented Westcrown's fate, as the capital was moved north to Egorian; no longer the City of Nine Stars, Westcrown now became the City of Twilight. The remains of the Chelaxian nobility huddle in Westcrown as a home-in-exile, as the city itself crumbles under the weight of mismanagement, crime, and a mysterious plague of creatures that hunt the streets at night.

In 4716 AR, Westcrown found itself the target of an uprising led by the Glorious Reclamation, an Iomedaean group of knights opposed to House Thrune.
1201890433:
Council Hall and Gaol Block

The Council Hall and Gaol Block in the city of Katapesh is a walled compound standing atop a low bluff overlooking the Lower City. It was known as Aktep Keep and was the estate of Massein Aktep an extremely wealthy businessman in Katapesh. When he was involved in a huge scandal, in 4701 AR, implicating him for tax evasion, fraud, and murdering of several prominent guildmasters, the Pactmasters seized all of his holdings and turned his expansive personal dungeon into the city's new gaol, where he is currently imprisoned. His estate has been converted into the Council Hall where the city's guildmasters and other officials meet. It is also houses the barracks for the Zephyr Guard.
1214503193:
Haldun

Haldun is a town on the southern edge of the nation of Rahadoum, situated on the main pass linking Rahadoum with the southern lands. At one time it was a thriving centre of trade, dealing with Lirgen and the other lands to the south. The advent of the Eye of Abendego destroyed Lirgen, turning the area into a hurricane-blasted wilderness.

As of 4711 AR, however, Haldun had become a fortress town guarding Rahadoum against incursions of monsters and cultists, and is the centre of the defensive network known as the Rainwall. Its inhabitants are hardy folk who take a perverse pride in their harsh lives, and see the devastation to the south as a warning as to what happens to those who trust their lives to deities.
1226962133:
Kuruban Swamp

Kuruban Swamp is a line of heavily wooded islands in Vudra's Open Bridge region that was not fully connected to the mainland when the Obari Ocean receded in 4606 AR. Instead, the upheaval created a semi-sunken mangrove swamp. Unlike the rest of Open Bridge, which is infested with hordes of horrific aberrations, Kuruban Swamp is controlled by sedacthys who ruthlessly protect its borders. The sedacthys seem to be searching amongst the ancient ruins that dot the swamp, but no one knows for what.
1230187813:
El-Shelad

El-Shelad is a small city on the Scorpion Coast of Osirion. It was once just a slave camp housing a part of the hordes of slaves that built Tumen. The city flourished under the Qadiran rulership of the country mainly because of the resulting influx of Keleshites that strengthened its economy.

El-Shelad is a city of fierce political struggle and racial tension. When the first Osirian ruler of the country after 3,000 years of Keleshite rulership, Khemet I, the Forthbringer, installed a loyal governor in El-Shelad, he tried to dissipate the political tension. He allowed the city's ruler to retain the traditional Qadiran title of "wali" but the struggle between the Osirian loyalists and Keleshite sympathizers has never ceased. The city is known for its stormy elections which until recently produced Garundi governors loyal to Sothis. Its current ruler, Sarita Senbi is Keleshite and thus distrusted by Garundi citizens.
1257082313:
Oppara

Oppara, the City of Empire, (pronounced oh-PAH-rah) is the capital of Taldor, and by far the largest city in the realm. It is an ancient and thriving city which earned the title the Gilded City because of the many roofs, spires, and domes that once glittered with gold plating in the high afternoon sun. Even though the metal was long ago stripped away, Oppara remains the example of what the empire once was and what it could be again, though, today, it is plagued with mismanagement, selfish aristocrats, poverty, and crime.
1260918943:
Ipeq

4 locations:
  • Eyes of the Bronze Man
  • Khopesh Span
  • Mount Osiki
  • Safani
1273880573:
Pangolais

4 locations:
  • Cathedral of Embodied Wisdom
  • Chainhouse
  • Hall of the Chronicles
  • Shrine of Convocations
1287358813:
Hessim, Newby, and Sage Paint Manufactory

1 locations:
  • Travelling Man
1302838293:
Akhenaten

Little is known about the ruins of Akhenaten in eastern Thuvia near its border with Osirion. The ruins are located close to the junction of the Junira and the Scarab Rivers. On the Osirian side of the Junira are the ruins of el-Amara.
1331505843:
High Isle

High Isle, along with its sister city of Low Isle, provides much of the seafood for the nation of Xopatl. Located along the south-western coast of Xopatl on a small island in Lake Tapipallati, High Isle is renowned for its seafood. The fishermen of High Isle go on weeks-long fishing trips into the lake's depths to hunt for giant catfish and other large lake monsters.
1339147643:
Cayden's Hall

Cayden's Hall in the Ascendant Court district of Absalom is a large, boisterous meadhall and temple of Cayden Cailean, built of wood as was the original meadhall from which Cayden set out to take the Test of the Starstone. Due to the drunken revelry, religious bonfires, and consequent accidents, this is the fourteenth temple built on the current site near a large fountain, which is itself at least the fourth site to house Cayden's temple.

Efforts are made by the local priests and the house guards to quell any serious fights and extinguish uncontrolled fires, despite the fact that they are often as inebriated as the patrons who frequent the hall. Aside from simple drinks and food, the hall also boasts a mid-sized speaking hall, intended for both religious proclamations and general entertainment.

Ruled by High Priestess Saphira, the Lady of Intemperance, it is not merely a place to get drunk, but also a gathering-hall for adventurers to meet and plot expeditions, rebellions, and exploits of all kinds.
1345912623:
Stom's Claim

Stom's Claim is a ramshackle silver mining camp deep in the Menador Mountains between Cheliax and Nidal.
1364660473:
Augur Temple

5 locations:
  • Drunken Tailor
  • Flesh Block
  • Haverston's Grange
  • Pinnacle
  • Stockyards
1365335633:
Roslar's Coffer

Roslar's Coffer was a small town located in southwestern Lastwall along the Tourondel River, near the border with the Hold of Belkzen.
1365652783:
Magnimar

26 locations:
  • Dome of the Savored Sting
  • Arvensoar
  • Deadeye Lodge
  • Defiant's Garden
  • Cathedral of Abadar
  • Church of Pharasma
  • Deverin Villa
  • Kaijitsu Townhouse
  • Temple of Iomedae
  • and 17 others…
1372107933:
Ten Thousand Summer Palace

In the time of Imperial Lung Wa, the Ten Thousand Summer Palace in southern Bachuan, on the northern eaves of the Chang Liao Jungle, was a favorite winter retreat for many emperors. The palace was an enormous walled compound tended by a vast staff of sorcerers and clerics. During the Bachuan Revolution, the palace was besieged by the revolutionary armies, but its spellcasting inhabitants shut its gates and blasted the rebellious peasants to ash. The palace's gates never opened again.

Although the revolution succeeded in taking over Bachuan, no infiltrators have entered the Ten Thousand Summer Palace and returned to tell the tale, with one single exception. A mercenary from Minkai emerged, covered in glowing sigils and clutching a gigantic emerald, only to die as he stumbled into a nearby village muttering the words, "...beautiuful...terrible..."
1385276913:
Thorn's End

Thorn's End is a hidden fortress of the Winter Council in the demon-infested Tanglebriar of Kyonin.
1401412793:
Fort Longjaw

Fort Longjaw is located northeast of Solku, near the Brazen Peaks. It is a wooden stockade that stands as a symbol against kholo aggression. Its proud red flag displays a bleached white jawbone divided by a silver sword. The fort's residents are desert warriors who dedicate themselves to driving back kholo raiding parties, destroying kholo settlements wherever they lurk, and hunting down any other desert dangers such as hyenas. Led by Giana Secondstride, every day they scout the area for miles, hunting for slaver tracks, setting up false caravans to act as decoys, and interrogating travelers about any signs of kholo activity.
1409786703:
Ketskerlet

Ketskerlet is the chief township of the Khorkii Clans, built into the Wall of Heaven where the mountains meet the Gulf of Khorkii. The stone halls of Ketskerlet provide shelter for the Erutaki sailors during the cold winter months. Fully a third of its population is made up of dwarves, who maintain the shelters during the summer.
1414962653:
Aerie of Bloodletting Songs

The plateau known as the Aerie of Bloodletting Songs stands 200 feet above the floor of the Mwangi Jungle. It was once the holy site of an now-extinct tribe of demon-worshipers. The death and suffering which took place here has warped the local animals and forest spirits, which the local Zenj tribes believe are manifestations of the trapped souls of those who died here so long ago.
1417275523:
Katrivish

Katrivish is a small town in Iobaria's Norinor forest inhabited by seemingly mad druids who worship the cyclops god Prathos, and hunt those who trespass in the woods around their home.
1429359743:
Three Rings Tavern

3 locations:
  • Bank of Abadar
  • North Bridge
  • Whitecaps
1439455923:
Arvensoar

Arvensoar is the tower built by local Varisians and the citizens of Magnimar to replace the Seacleft Spire after it was destroyed by an angelic being. It took over 18 years to create the tower, but it now stands as a symbol of the city's receptiveness to the guidance of good spirits.

At approximately 400 feet in height, the tower is the tallest structure in Magnimar and stands over the districts of Lowcleft and Naos. The tower serves as the barracks and fortress for the local city watch and is defended with eight powerful trebuchets able to target any attackers regardless of the direction from which they attack.
1446753503:
Derexhi Villa

Derexhi Villa is the seat of House Derexhi, one of the Varisian city-state of Magnimar's founding families. Located in the upscale Alabaster District, it was run by the family's head, Randred Derexhi, who was a member of the Justice Council.
1455064733:
Safani

The settlement of Safani is a small town near the Crook River, in Osirion. Its leader is Efni Raan.

Safani is too far from major cities to receive the benefit of their protection against kholo raiders, so the town's residents came up with a unique defense strategy. The town is fully camouflaged: the buildings are built into the hills and covered in sod. Other vegetation has been carefully cultivated so it can cover more conspicuous aspects of the buildings, like doors and windows.
1456620413:
Lopul

Lopul is a large caravan city in the southern desert of Qadira.
1472040543:
Tumen

Tumen was once Osirion's largest city and briefly its capital. Located 75 miles from Sothis in the eastern Underdunes far from the River Sphinx, the city relied upon pure water drawn forth "by the grace of the Opaline Satrap whom the Radiant Pharaoh had favored, and in turn was favored by", which contemporary Osirionologists speculate, referred to a massive aquifer or portal to the elemental planes. The city's water supply began to ebb shortly before the deaths of the Four Pharaohs of Ascension, and the city descended to abandonment and an endless cycle of burial in the khamsin sandstorms. The lifeblood of the city was the geyser that erupted from a vertical crater in the mountain, providing its inhabitants with drinking water. The pharaohs used a figurine-shaped artifact, imbued with magic, to make the geyser erupt, though it became inactive after their deaths. The artifact possesses its own consciousness, and if it were to be reactivated, it could once again make life easier in the ruined city, though it would likely begin plotting its own schemes now that its masters are gone.

Despite its remote location, the city remains a popular destination for scholars and treasure hunters.
1474945373:
Deepgate

Deepgate is a huge stone and iron fortress located deep in the Kodar Mountains of the Hold of Belkzen, to the east of the source of the Kazaron River.
1480716573:
Ferny

The village of Ferny is the largest settlement of shoonies in the Swardlands of the Isle of Kortos.
1489208783:
Nightfall Station

Nightfall Station is the most profitable of the Aspis Consortium trading posts in the Mwangi Expanse. It is thirteen days' travel by wheelboat against the current from Bloodcove (assuming the travelers push on for a while after dark on the last day and arrive at the station at night, hence the name) but the return journey with the current is only five days.
1490898883:
Ground of Lost Tears

The Ground of Lost Tears, located in the Ustalavic county of Odranto, is the site of a brutal massacre that occurred during the Demonskin War.
1520615773:
Zulran's Equines

Zulran's Equines is a sprawling horse ranch belonging to An'Aldane Zulran, guildmaster of the Imperial Union of Breeders. His horses and camels are regarded as some of the finest in all of Katapesh, if not the entire Inner Sea. The horsemaster's animals are bred for one thing: speed, and for this reason they are greatly valued by collectors. Zulran's reputation for breeding fast horses is matched only by his reputation for being a legendary haggler, marking his horses up to well above the usual rate.

The ranch is also a profitable riding school. An'Aldane's riding school is shockingly expensive but well worth the price, as those who complete his school ride away with unique and enviable skills.

A recent theft of one of his finest chargers by a kholo bandit named Bristlemane has left An'Aldrane infuriated and offering a 1,000-gold-piece reward for anyone than can return the horse and the head of the offender.
1526569553:
Urchin's Maw

Located in the Arcadian Ocean west of Acisazi and northwest of the Dismal Nitch, the entrance to the underwater cave known as Urchin's Maw gapes from the side of the continental shelf, overlooking a precipitous drop into the inky depths below. It earns its name from the riot of sizable deep sea urchins that dwell on the rock face surrounding the cave entrance.
1533419433:
Reiver's Right

Reiver's Right is a former logging camp that has been long abandoned. The former loggers here became victims of a forest blight and were transformed into trees, and the blight still remains here.
1537312533:
Tok-Tshka

Tok-Tshka, also known as the Evernest, is a bog strider gathering place in the depths of the Verduran Forest about a day-and-a-half's travel from Bellis. It is not quite a community but is, instead, a place for the solitary creatures to assemble. Surrounded by hills, Tok-Tshka consists of a small, nameless lake with a trio of islands sat in the middle of it. The islands are linked by woven marsh grass and their tree canopies. At the heart of the largest island sits a grove of evergreen trees surrounding a small pond with a boulder at its centre where the bog striders convene meetings with outsiders. Tok-Tshka is led by a seer-queen known as Rik'Visk.
1537651773:
Ruba's Shop

Ruba's Shop is a scrivener's shop in the Old Kintargo district of Kintargo that also serves as a notary and translator. Unbeknownst to most, the owner—the elderly Varisian, Ruba Fenquay—is also an accomplished forger whose other illegal sideline business is copying forbidden texts.
1538727523:
Sea King Shipyards

Sea King Shipyards is a small shipyard in the Docks district of the city of Absalom. The yard builds only one ocean-going vessel at a time, but it remains famous for the high quality and sturdiness of its fluyts and galleons that can sail the most dangerous waters in the Inner Sea.
1539957843:
Brimstone Springs

Brimstone Springs is a small settlement high in the Mindspin Mountains of Nidal known for its hot springs: the Soulsheen Baths. Hundreds of people from all over the region come every year to soak in its sulfur-rich mineral waters that fall in natural cascades, giving the town a rather unpleasant smell. These waters then collect to form the source of the Sulfur River, which flows westward out of the mountains toward Usk Lake. Brimstone Springs has also gained a certain amount of notoriety from the golden dream, a special, day-long immersion in poisonous waters that, if it doesn't kill the user, gives him or her visions of the afterlife while permanently dying the skin a deep yellow.
1547538283:
Mithril Hall

The Mithril Hall is a beautiful, traditional-style structure in Westgate that serves as offices and residences for the bureaucrats and diplomats of the Western Council, which administers the district. It is divided into hexagonal sections, resembling a honeycomb, with each office having a corridor that connects to an interior courtyard with a garden and fountain in the center. While undeniably beautiful, it can be disorienting for visitors. The Hall is open five days a week until sunset and later on weekends, and this schedule has drawn complaints from working-class residents who find it difficult to attend to administrative matters or file complaints during those hours.
1556602543:
Broken Hook

Broken Hook is the southernmost tip of Taldas Isle in the Shackles, south of the town of Little Oppara. It derives its name from the way the craggy land breaks off into countless small rocks and islets and trails off into the sea. The children of Little Oppara play here, daring each other to see who can hop farthest away from land while jumping from boulder to boulder. They also claim that the Broken Hook is home to an oceanic giant, but as of 4712 AR, this had been dismissed as a fairy tale until a spate of recent disappearances.
1567385203:
Qintong

Qintong is a city in the hobgoblin nation of Kaoling that features a system of social mobility for its non-hobgoblin residents, a unique program that is controversial among the nation's ruling Council of Nine. It is ruled by Sun Zhen, a member of the Council.
1580970803:
Iz

The city of Iz (pronounced IZ) was the first to fall to the demonic forces which poured out of the Worldwound in 4606 AR.
1592337953:
Blakros Museum

The Blakros Museum was the former stronghold of a mad astrologer and wizard named Ralzeros the Overwatched in the early history of Absalom. The mage constructed the building from a solid block of volcanic stone with the aid of dark, alien powers. After his disappearance, the building was eventually bought by the Blakroses, a matriarchal family of influential nobles and artifact hunters, who filled the building with relics and artifacts collected from throughout the Inner Sea region.
1592966873:
Succoring Muse

3 locations:
  • Broken Monastery
  • Halfway Houses
  • Silent Partner
1593806113:
Terapasillion

Terapasillion (which means "Ziggurat of Night" in the Shadowtongue) is an ancient step pyramid eternally cloaked in shadows that predates Taldan settlement in the region, and is located on the southern North Plains of Ravounel near Nidal. It is rumored to have been built by shadow giants during the Age of Darkness, but was more recently inhabited by an umbral dragon. Despite the fact that the dragon has not been seen in decades, locals still avoid the place with a passion.
1596110033:
Escadar

Escadar is a city on the Isle of Erran off the north coast of the Isle of Kortos. It houses a city-sized military outpost, acting as one of Absalom's primary bases of naval operations.
1604207123:
Solifugid Nest

Beneath the Lady's Lagoon lies the Solifugid Nest, a cavern filled with spores and fungi where these strange arachnids reside. A pillar in the center depicts a beautiful woman with a barely legible inscription; it is hollow inside and once contained the succubus Avalexi.
1637423783:
Vellumis

Vellumis was the oldest and largest city in the nation of Lastwall, and remains the largest bastion of the Knights of Lastwall in the Gravelands since the nation was destroyed by Tar-Baphon in 4719 AR.

Situated on the shores of Lake Encarthan, it served as the port of embarkation and debarkation for soldiers traveling in and out of the country on their way to keep watch over the terrors of Ustalav and the Hold of Belkzen, then as the evacuation point for refugees fleeing the nation after 4719 AR.
1647661683:
Littletown

As of 4710 AR, Littletown was a modest community located in Pitax in the River Kingdoms.
1662028133:
Irogath Monastery

Irogath Monastery is located at the foot of the Mindspin Mountains, where Kuthite monks reside and train in ascetic scarification. The complex is designed to facilitate this discipline, with undecorated corridors, beds without pillows, and short benches—everything constructed to help the monks contemplate the suffering of life.

Those pilgrims wishing to enter the inner chambers must obtain the approval of Mistress Merinda the Striped, who is said to be able to discern a person's devotion to Zon-Kuthon simply by looking into their eyes. The trials to be endured in these inner chambers are agonizing, and the few who succeed never speak of what they gained upon completing the journey.
1662567523:
Pegasi Bridges

The Pegasi Bridges were built many years ago, consisting of eight bridges with bas-reliefs and sculptures of pegasi on their sides, serving to cross the Canaroden. Most of these bridges have been destroyed over time, with only two remaining standing today.
1665130853:
Chalya

Chalya is a city located in the Lohaparbat Mountains of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra. Chalya is the most easily accessible city by land for those visiting the Falling Mountains region, and is home to numerous pagodas and ashrams that draw visitors from across Vudra.
1680113493:
Nightshade Theater

Nightshade Theater is a stone structure that, from the outside, resembles a cathedral dedicated to some malevolent god more than a theater, but inside it boasts silk carpets, paintings of macabre scenes, and seating for around 250 people. It was created by Lord Mayor Aberian Arvanxi, who sought to enhance the city's macabre theatrical culture with this investment.
1685866983:
Natatorium

The natatorium of Nal-Kashel, an island in the Hellmouth Gulf west of Cheliax off the shore of Blackcove, was a stepped public pool once used by the island's Azlanti inhabitants during the era of ancient Azlant. Like the rest of the abandoned island, it is now in ruins.
1715504513:
Bailer's Retreat

3 locations:
  • Citadel Volshyenek
  • Creaky Hammock
  • Eodred's Walk
1731967543:
Yanti Iñulde

Yanti Iñulde is a city within Xopatl that is renowned as the nation's religious centre. Yanti Iñulde was the home town of the last hero-god to be born in Xopatl back in the nation's ancient history. From this Yanti Iñulde rose as a centre for religious worship of the hero-gods and other deities beyond that, becoming a city riddled with temples and shrines, some so old that the deities they were built to honour have been completely forgotten. The city holds a week-long festival to celebrate all the deities worshipped within the city, including those whose memory has been all but lost to time.
1732505753:
Thistletop

The island of Thistletop is located in the Varisian Gulf off the coast of the Nettlewood.
1746076383:
Fort Faelon

Fort Faelon is an abandoned Nirmathi fort. Located along the coast north from Tamran, this fort overlooks the waters of Lake Encarthan from the marshy area where it was built.
1774555003:
Colvaas Gibbet

The small river port of Colvaas Gibbet is located on the Mainland area of the Shackles, approximately 25 miles northeast of Port Peril.
1787196283:
Orkatto's Feathers and Fur

Orkatto's Feathers and Fur caters to those nouveau riche of Korvosa who have a taste for exotic pets. The unique store is located in the newer South Shore district of the Varisian city. The store is a strange wonderland of exotic animals, from caged songbirds to gem-hued snakes, all the way up to dream spiders which are valued by Orkatto's more shady clientele for their venom, which can be refined into a powerful drug called shiver. The store's owner, Orkatto, served as a well-respected master of the hunt to a powerful lord in the nearby city of Magnimar, before finally saving enough money to open his own namesake store.
1789713103:
Fort Benbem

3 locations:
  • Heavywater Perch
  • Moak Harbor
  • Wailing Cave
1791160263:
Inverted Tower

The Inverted Tower is a strange, inverted structure atop a crevice in the Mindspin Mountains. The tower has been magically enchanted to reverse gravity, allowing those who enter to walk upside down. Powerful divination magic is amplified by the tower's properties and is used by its sole resident, Fisla Dierks, to enhance her harrow deck reads.
1793292543:
Storval Stairs

Built thousands of years ago by the enslaved giants of the Thassilonian empire, the Storval Stairs are a set of stair-like tiers carved into the side of the western Storval Rise, then part of Shalast. Although sized for a gigantic creature, they are by far the easiest way to cross the Rise west of the Iron Peaks. It is not uncommon for the Storval Rise to reach heights of over 1,000 feet in many places, sharply delineating the fertile lowlands from the barren scrub land of the Storval Plateau. At the Storval Stairs, however, the Rise is only 400 feet in height with human-sized steps and ramps cut into the gargantuan ones, making the passage even easier. Immense statues of the runelord Karzoug flank the stairs, although the southern-most one is beginning to finally crumble due to erosion.
1795559503:
Klarwa Fountain

The Klarwa Fountain is located in the desert of western Osirion, in the region known as the Footprints of Rovagug. The site is a bowl-shaped depression filled with rain water atop a steep cinder cone from a long-extinct volcano, which rises hundreds of feet above the surrounding area. The water never quite evaporates despite the heat, and gases occasionally vent from deep within the cone. The violent plumes of water are sometimes visible from miles away, and one such column drew the attention of the anchorite Klarwa as he was dying of thirst in the badlands during the Second Age, giving the landmark its name.

As of 4714 AR the hill is used by the few travelers making their way through western Osirion, and its water makes the ascent up the steep hill well worth the effort. Some hermits live within sight of the Klarwa Fountain and hike up its sides when driven by desperate thirst, as anchorite holy Klarwa was.
1815756043:
M'neri Valley Cattle Ranch

M'neri Valley Cattle Ranch began as a cattle ranch on the edge of the M'neri Plains in Sargava. It was established by Olgran Macini under the name Freehold as a holiday spot, but has since grown into the equivalent of a small town. Olgan always tried to be fair to the native Mwangi ranch hands, and since then they have been paid a fair wage and offered a share of the profits.

It is currently run by Olgran's great-great-granddaughter Mindra Macini, although her cousin Salgarth is trying to gain control. He would like to run it as a "normal" Sargavan business, without any benefits for the Mwangi workers.

In the aftermath of Sargava's fall and the establishment of Vidrian, Freehold was renamed to M'neri Cattle Ranch, while Mindra appointed two native siblings who work as a herder and breeder to handle daily operations. Some members of the Combined Mercantile Interests and Field Unions say Mindra should waive all of her claims if she believes the Mwangi to be her equals, while certain people who wish to bring back colonial rule seek to harshly punish Mindra and her ranch.
1817284303:
Raliscrad

Raliscrad was a city located at the confluence of the Sarkora and Isk Rivers in the region of Sarkoris (formerly the Worldwound) known as the Riftshadow.
1818755833:
Whisperfall Pass

Whisperfall Pass is the largest and most well-known pass through the southern Tusk Mountains, connecting the Hold of Belkzen to the Ustalavic county of Canterwall. Because of the Belkzen orcs' love of raiding human lands, it is also one of the most contested areas in the country, with the Skull Eater tribe controlling its eastern slopes while the Blood Trail tribe dominates in the west.

1832560133:
Mount Kazunan

Mount Kazunan is a volcano in central Minkai along the Kyojin Mountains west of the Ningyo Gulf. It is one of the Three Fires, along with Mount Kumijinja in the Kamifushi Mountains and Mount Yogan in the southern Kyojin Mountains.
1844839833:
Trackless Storm

The Trackless Storm is an endless stretch of sandstorm in southern Katapesh near the border with Nex. The trackless storm rages year-round regardless of the surrounding weather conditions, although the severity of the storm varies. Sometimes the Trackless Storm is little more than a mild dust cloud, while other times it blows strong enough to scour flesh from bone. The landscape within the storm constantly changes as the winds alter the sand dunes.

Numerous rumours and legends are connected with the Trackless Storm. Some claim that a field of hardy, untouched pesh lies at the heart of the storm, while others claim that the Stone Sorcerer's legendary Sanctum of Sand resides within a valley hidden inside its swirling winds.
1857414513:
Cradle of Immortality

The Cradle of Immortality, located in the Tian Xia nation of Lingshen far to the north of the Golden River, is where artisans have sculpted terracotta likenesses of soldiers serving King Huang. His wizards have connected the statues to the souls of the warriors with the underworld dragon's blessing. Whenever one of these warriors dies, their soul is transferred to the terra-cotta soldier to continue serving their lord until the simulacrum is destroyed.

The Cradle, which is a series of long structures arranged in a cluster, is surrounded by hundreds of clay quarries. Craftspeople sculpt the constructs from the clay inside of the Cradle, all similar in general shape, form, and equipment, and are arranged into groups for various ancestries.

None has witnessed the harvesting of these souls on battlefields, and the process of collecting them and animating the soldiers is unknown not only to the public but also to all who work in the Cradle except its most senior laborers. The necromancer Laochong, the "Old Worm" as nicknamed by King Huang, is responsible for the soul-binding magic and uses the pricked blood collected when each soldier swears their oath to the crown. Not even King Huang knows Laochong's true nature or identity.

A large cavern beneath the Cradle serves as a repository for souls to be installed into the terra-cotta forms. Record keepers track the souls, including the few that silently but knowingly go unaccounted for.
1875411133:
Restov

Restov is one of the two largest cities in the fertile region of Rostland in southern Brevoy. It is known as the Free City of Restov, but it is allied with Brevoy.
1876908443:
Renchurch

The haunted fortress-monastery of Renchurch in the damned Ustalavic province of Virlych is one of the headquarters of the undead-obsessed Whispering Way. It is overseen by the Gray Friar, a powerful huecuva cleric of Urgathoa.

1900924473:
Mount Osiki

Mount Osiki is the tallest mountain within the range of Brazen Peaks in northern Garund, east of the Pahmet settlement of Erekrus. Its foothills and smaller peaks extend almost to the Crook River.

An ancient stairway begins from a stone landing on the southern bank of the Crook, winding through the foothills and the peaks to end at the top of the mountain. Upon the peak is an ancient shrine to a forgotten deity, and the few people that climb the ancient stairway each year are rumored to have committed to the pilgrimage after being saved from death or ruin by a dark-cloaked stranger. Nothing is known about what happens at the shrine, as the pilgrims returning from the top of Mount Osiki do not talk about it under any inducement.
1920450453:
Temple of the Sunken Sign

The Temple of the Sunken Sign is a Ghol-Gani temple complex dedicated to Azathoth, hidden in the Sodden Lands.
1924977723:
Riverford

Riverford is a small Andoren agricultural community situated up the Andoshen River from the capital of Almas.
1926065223:
Rickety's Squibs

Rickety's Squibs is a village at the mouth of a nameless river on the Slithering Coast in the Shackles' mainland. It is concealed from passing ships by a protruding headland and jungle, and is difficult to find if one does not know where to look.

Rickety's Squibs refits and modifies the lines and profiles of ships to provide a new identity for a price. These rebuilt vessels are referred to as "squibs." Rickety's Squibs is run by Rickety Hake, a retired pirate; he was the former mate of the Bearded Whore, whose Captain Ella Gurnett was killed at the battle of Nolis Point.

As of 4720 AR, rumors suggested that the Chelaxian Navy had taken over Rickety's Squibs in order to coerce its crew into refitting a warship in such a manner that it could defeat Hurricane Queen Tessa Fairwind's Luck of the Draw in battle.
1940608573:
Illmarsh

Illmarsh, formerly called Baytown, is a town in the southern Ustalavic province of Versex. It is rumored that a significant fraction of the population is a member of the Old Cults.
1949517873:
Haven

Haven is a clouded island in the middle of Lake Ocota that contains the largest settlement of Kallijae elves. The fog that surrounds the island serves the Kallijae well, as they prefer highly defensible and hard-to-find areas to settle. Because of how hard it is for others to find, Haven serves as a place to retreat to and recuperate. The Kallijae sometimes call it Last Stand to reflect this.
1966013083:
Wyvernsting

Wyvernsting, formerly called Wyvern's Sting, is the second largest settlement in Belkzen by population. It is located in the north-easternmost spur of the Kodar Mountains, surrounded by a high wooden stockade. It controls one of the Belkzen's rare forests and is close to the valuable and dangerous beasts of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords to the north, bringing wealth and power to its rulers.

As of 4724 AR, Unbroken Horn Hold chief Paskis Nine-Knives rules Wyvernsting. She succeeded her predecessor Hundux Half-Man around 4716 AR after overthrowing and executing him in a three-day-long coup.

Lost Mines

The Lost Mines once dug deep into the roots of the Mindspin Mountains on the western border of Nirmathas. They, and all their miners, suddenly disappeared in an unexplained occurrence.

1988874963:
Nine Walls

Nine Walls is a heavily defended nation of Matanji orcs located in the Mwangi Expanse. Its name is derived from its nine concentric cities, each ringed in tall, fortified, hexagonally shaped walls. Despite being beset by demons throughout its existence, the inner sanctums of Nine Walls are among the safest locations of the Expanse as well as some of the most cultured centers of orc civilization in the Inner Sea region.
1999673673:
Margruel's Masts

Margruel's Masts is a popular shipyard in The Docks, building cutters and caravels that serve a variety of purposes for both sailors and merchants.

2021189233:
Pavilion of Falling Flowers

The Pavilion of Falling Flowers is the primary academy of Jininese swordfighting and magical combat. Admission is highly selective, especially for outsiders.
2023697863:
Grimsburrow

As of 4712 AR, the Regulate of Grimsburrow was a newly constructed independent dwarven settlement in western Druma, north of the end of the Five Kings Range. Its residents were master crafters from across dwarven kingdoms who had traveled to follow a so-called renaissance in engineering, architecture, and art led by Brond, the commune's leader.

2062560273:
Karcau

The city of Karcau (pronounced KAHR-cow) is the only major population center in the land of Sinaria in Ustalav. It is renowned as a shining light of civilisation in a region of dark, dreary wilderness. The city is most famous for the Karcau Opera and has a reputation as a place that both produces musical genius and fosters it, it is this reputation that has lead to Karcau's nickname of the Village of Voices. Karcau is nestled on the banks of Lake Prophyria and its waters cut their way through the city, criss-crossing it in various canals. These canals mirror the situation below the city as Karcau was built atop an underground lake and its foundations are shot through with underwater tunnels creating a mostly submerged labyrinth.
2063192773:
Misarias

The city of Misarias in northeastern Cheliax stands on the banks of the River Keld on the Isgeri border, and serves as a waypoint for travelers of all sorts—thieves to diplomats, and brigands to scouts. With a suspicious eye on Andoran and Molthune, and with memories of the Goblinblood Wars ever in mind, Her Infernal Majestrix's generals keep most of the nation's massive army stationed in the region, where they could hold back an invasion ere reinforcements could come across the Fields of Chelam or the Sirmium Plains to aid them. Despite the large military presence, rumors hold that agents of the Molthune Armsrunners, a mercenary group operating out of Logas in Isger, keep rooms in Misarias where the organization is recruiting guards, customers, and scouts within Cheliax's borders.

2080196333:
Aroden's Eye

Aroden's Eye is a rocky mountain with a circular hole 150 feet wide in the northeastern World's Edge Mountains of Taldor, east of the Thousand Relics Pass. It was named due to the similarity between its shape and Aroden's religious symbol, and was a frequent pilgrimage site for Arodenites, enough to support a small monastery consisting of twelve structures connected by rope bridges and narrow stairways.

After Aroden's death, the pilgrimages stopped, tithes no longer came, and the now-powerless clerics of Aroden abandoned the temple, except the old, bitter Leomaris Gurgin, who converted to the worship of Groetus. The desecrated temple has since then been inhabited by the Last Acolytes, a clan of harpies whom Gurgin converted to his new faith, and a handful of lunar nagas who serve as their astrologers. They sacrifice captives and recklessly summon murderous proteans, attracting redcaps and animate dreams in the process. Travellers report nightmares, with eerily similar descriptions of the mountain and a howling moon.
2087226743:
Gundrun

Gundrun is a recently reoccupied town in the revived nation of Sarkoris, located in the area previously referred to as the Worldwound. The people of Gundrun are descendants of the original inhabitants of the area, back when it was known as Sarkoris.
2089338143:
Castle Urion

Castle Urion is the first town most crusaders heading to Mendev up the West Sellen River see when the enter Numeria, but it is far from the typical barbarian settlements that are strewn across the country. This settlement is technically under the control of the Black Sovereign, but it is in reality controlled by an order of knights dedicated to Iomedae. These knights built Castle Urion as a way to protect and guide those crusaders sailing up the River Sellen (or "River Road" as it is often called) from Cassomir in Taldor to Chesed. This order of Iomedaean knights is particularly special, as they ride trained griffons, which help them patrol vast stretches of the river, certainly more than they could if they patrolled only on horseback. The current commander of Castle Urion is Caroliss Minerran.

2123891853:
Ruined Monastery

The Ruined Monastery is an abandoned temple dedicated to Lamashtu in Katapesh. It was built during the reign of the kholos, though it is now in disrepair and is only visited for its eerie atmosphere as a place of worship for this ill-fated goddess. Several guides offer tours of the building for a small fee.
2136954533:
Vinpinna

Vinpinna is a town in the foothills of the Pardaparbat Mountains in the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra. Located in the south-western reaches of the Narhari Desert region, the nearby towns of Vinpinna and Adhaarm are each controlled by an order of undead martial artists.
2146251733:
Pobashabla

Pobashabla is a city-state in the underwater nation of Xidao, in the Xidao Gulf of eastern Tian Xia. It is located in Western Xidao, the part west of the Aya-Maru trench, and is sometimes referred to as the "western capital" of Xidao.

The city is dominated by the presence of the Xidao Army, which influences the city's culture but also generates tension with separatists who oppose them as an occupying force.
